sections.heading: 1. Inline Functions
sections.content: When a function is marked as inline, the compiler copies its body (and lambda arguments) directly into the call site instead of performing a traditional function call. This avoids lambda object creation and improves performance.

sections.heading: Inline Function Example
sections.content: Avoids lambda allocation by pasting code at the call site.

sections.heading: Why Use Inline?
sections.content: Every lambda passed to a non-inline function creates a Function object. Inside loops, this can create memory pressure and increase garbage collection frequency.

sections.heading: 2. noinline and crossinline
sections.content: Inline functions allow control over lambda behavior using noinline and crossinline.

sections.heading: Lambda Modifiers
items: noinline: Keeps a lambda as a normal object so it can be stored or passed around.
crossinline: Prevents non-local returns when the lambda is executed in another context.

sections.heading: 3. Reified Type Parameters
sections.content: Normally, generic types are erased at runtime. Inline functions enable reified type parameters, allowing access to the actual type at runtime.

sections.heading: Reified Example
sections.content: Starting an Activity without passing .class manually.

sections.heading: 4. Value Classes (Inline Classes)
sections.content: Value classes provide type safety for wrapping a single value without allocating a full object at runtime.

sections.heading: Value Class Example
sections.content: Acts like a wrapper but compiles down to the underlying type.

sections.heading: 5. When to Use Inline
sections.content: Inline improves performance when used correctly but increases generated bytecode size.

sections.heading: Best Practices
items: Use inline for functions that accept lambdas.
Use inline when you need reified type parameters.
Avoid inline for very large functions.
Avoid inline for functions without lambdas.

sections.heading: Summary
items: inline pastes code at the call site to reduce allocations.
reified allows runtime access to generic types.
value class provides type safety without runtime object cost.
Inline features are widely used in Android libraries.
